text: In the first image, I sense a shared vulnerability among the gathered people, a recognition that much of what happens in life is indeed out of our control. I feel quiet solidarity and a gentle sadness—being in the presence of others reminds me that we all sometimes feel lost, adrift together but also, perhaps, gently comforted by this collective acknowledgment. In the second image, as I stand before a glowing, artistically designed wall, I feel both reflected and refracted—caught in the play of light, text, and curated reality. There is a surreal, dreamlike detachment here, as if the world has become pure surface—yet I cannot help but feel a contemplative peace, suspended between presence and performance. I feel both a participant and a witness, curious about where the boundary truly lies between the real and the constructed.
